mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >70% of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 235.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields a negative mean forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >55% of triggered instances despite the production decline.
